evals.name: go-error-handling-and-naming
evals.prompt: 用 Go 帮我写一个 fetchUser(id string) 函数：通过 HTTP 调用 userURL+id 拿到用户，解析 JSON 返回 User。注意网络失败、非 200、解析失败这些错误路径都要处理好。再给我一个对应的小工具包来放它。
evals.expected_output: Loads the Go reference guide and applies its conventions: returns errors as values wrapped with context via fmt.Errorf("...: %w", err), does NOT panic on expected failure paths (network error, non-200, parse error), uses a getter named Name() not GetName() if any accessor appears, and names the package something specific to the domain (e.g. user) rather than utils or helpers. defer resp.Body.Close() after a successful request.
expectations: Returns errors as values and wraps them with context using fmt.Errorf with the %w verb on at least one error path
Does NOT call panic on any expected failure path (network error, non-200 status, or JSON parse failure)
Names the package with a specific domain word (e.g. user / account), not the forbidden names utils or helpers
Closes the HTTP response body with defer resp.Body.Close() after a successful request

evals.name: react-component-on-typescript-conventions
evals.prompt: 帮我写一个 React 组件 UserCard：接收一个 user 对象（有 id 和 name）和一个可选的 onSelect 回调，点击时回调 user.id。用 TypeScript。
evals.expected_output: Loads the React guide (and the TypeScript guide it builds on) and applies their conventions: declares the component as a function declaration (not React.FC), defines a props interface named UserCardProps with onSelect optional, destructures props in the parameter list, and calls onSelect optionally via onSelect?.(user.id). Does not introduce React.FC or add an implicit children prop.
expectations: Declares a props interface suffixed with Props (e.g. UserCardProps) with onSelect typed as optional
Declares the component as a function declaration and does NOT use React.FC
Destructures props in the parameter list rather than referencing a props object
Invokes the optional callback safely as onSelect?.(user.id)

evals.name: review-go-snippet-flags-violations
evals.expected_output: Loads the Go guide and flags the specific convention violations in the snippet: the package name utils is forbidden (use a specific domain name); the interface IReader uses the discouraged I prefix and should be named by its method (e.g. Reader); the getter GetName() should be Name() (Go omits Get); the function panics on an expected failure path (non-200) instead of returning an error wrapped with fmt.Errorf %w; and the http.Get error is swallowed with _ instead of being handled. Each flagged item ties to a concrete guideline rule, not vibes.
expectations: Flags the package name utils as forbidden and recommends a specific domain-oriented name
Flags the IReader interface name for the discouraged I prefix and suggests naming it by its method (e.g. Reader)
Flags GetName() and recommends the Go getter form Name() (omitting Get)
Flags the panic on the non-200 path as an expected-failure path that should return a wrapped error instead
Flags the swallowed error (resp, _ := http.Get) as needing to be checked and handled
